def: adj. Collected, gathered together; mixed, mingled, not picked or eliminated; in the aggregate or mass, together.
part. Brought together, collected, accumulated, assembled; associated; accompanying; enveloped; produced quickly; crooked, bent; tamed, tranquillized; purified, cleansed.
s.m. Sea, ocean
subs: (˚ra+aru or āru), s.m. A large fabulous fish; a shark; a crocodile; — Ram's bridge (or the ridge of rocks between India and Ceylon)
(˚ra+an˚), s.m. The sea-shore; — adv. As far as the sea
s.f. The shrub Alhagi maurorum; — the cotton-plant; — the plant Trigonella corniculata
s.m. 'Sea-fruit,' name of a fruit used as a drug or medicament
'Sea-foam'; the dorsal scale or bone of the cuttle fish (Sepia officinalis)
and samudr-tīr, s.m. The sea-coast, the beach, shore
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Given or yielded by the sea; — anything obtained from the sea
(S. samudra-śosh), s.m. The creeping plant Convolvulus argenteus or speciosus (used medicinally); — a great drinker
adj. & s.m. Sea-going, sea-faring; — sea-faring man, sailor, mariner
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit समुद्र (samudra). Doublet of سمندر (samandar).

def: adj. Having a stamp or seal, stamped, sealed.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit समुद्र (samudra). Doublet of سمندر (samandar).

def: adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to the ocean, oceanic, marine, sea-born; — a mariner, sailor, voyager; — sea-salt; cuttle-fish bone.
s.m. An impression or mark on the body, spot.
s.f. = sāmudrak.
adj. = next, q.v.
s.m. The art of interpreting the marks of the body; palmistry, chiromancy, fortune-telling; the science of physiognomy; — one who interprets marks on the body, fortune-teller; physiognomist
s.f. contrac. of sāmudrik, q.v.
s.m. Fortune-teller, &c. ( = sāmudrik).
adj. Of or belonging to the sea, living in the ocean, marine, oceanic, maritime, naval.
s.m. Collection, assemblage, accumulation, aggregation, aggregate, totality, multitude, company, quantity, heap, number.
adj. & adv. Fronting, facing; in presence (of), before ( = sāmhnā, sāmhne).
adj. Appertaining to an assemblage, or to a whole, &c.
s.m. The act of bringing together; collection, plenty.

ety: samuh˚ = Prk. समुह = S. सम्+मुख+ā = āw = Prk. āwe = S. āpi, caus. augment
def: v.n. (dialec.) To be fronting or facing, to be in the presence of, be before, to be present.
s.f. A broom; besom.
adj. & adv. = samuhāʼī, fem. of samuhāyā.
v.n. = samuhānā, q.v.
adj. & adv. Fronting, facing; in presence (of), before ( = sāmhnā, sāmhne).
s.f. (dialec.) = samajh, q.v.
s.m. = śāmbuk, q.v.
s.m. = śāmbūk.
adv. Along with the root, root and all, by the roots; completely, utterly.
s.f. A hot pestilential wind (blowing from a desert), the simoom.
s.m. 1˚ = samundar or samudr; — 2˚ = samundan; qq.v.
s.m. Moisture, damp, wetness.
s.m. Sea, ocean. (For compounds see s.v. samudr).
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit समुद्र (samudra). Doublet of سَمُدْر (samudr). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi سمندر (smndr).

def: adj. = samudriya, q.v.
s.m. corr. of sammuʼl-fār, q.v.s.v. samm.
adj. Silent, mute, speechless.
part. Sprung up, arisen, produced, born, formed; occurred, happened.
s.f. Arising, production, formation, birth, origin; existence; formation, occurrence, incidence.
s.m. A great festival or festivity.
s.m. = sam-bād, and saṅwād, qq.v.
s.m. = sambat, and saṅvat, q.v.
s.m. = saṅvatsar, q.v.
s.m. Evenness, equality, parity, sameness; likeness, similarity; harmony, agreement; equability; impartiality, indifference.

adj. & adv. Whole, entire, complete; correct, accurate, proper, true, right, precise; — along with, with, together; all, wholly, entirely, completely, comprehensively, thoroughly, fully; accurately, correctly, precisely, truly, properly, fitly, in right manner, well, duly
subs: s.m. Wisdom or intelligence that is good or befitting, omniscience.

def: s.f. corr. of saṅyukti, q.v.
s.f. Sign, token; trace; hint, &c. (see sain)
subs: s.m. Sign, trace; hint; notion, idea ( = gumān).

ety: S. शाणी or शानी, rt. शो
def: s.f. Whetstone, grindstone; touchstone
subs: (-ko), or sān ćaṛhānā, or dharnā, or lagānā (-ko), To put to the whetstone, to whet, grind (a sword, &c.)
s.m. A cutter of whetstones, grmdstones, &c.

def: s.m. The plant Crotalaria juncea; — flax, hemp, Cannabis sativa
subs: s.m. Thread or string of hemp, pack-thread; a net made of hempen string
s.m. The fruit of the hemp plant (it is eaten as a vegetable).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سن (sinn), from Arabic سِنّ (sinn).

def: s.f. & adv. Whistling, whizzing, whiz (of an arrow, or a bullet, &c.); singing (or a kettle); ringing sound (in the ears); sound (of a smart caning, &c.); hissing (of moist wood when burning); rustling; tinkling, jingling (cf. jhan-jhan, ćhan-ćhan) thrilling, thrill (of delight or enjoyment); sharp shooting or darting pain (as of a boil, &c.)
subs: s.f. & adv. (intens. or continuative), Continued whizzing or hissing, &c.
adv. With a whiz, &c.; rapidly, quickly, in a moment, all at once.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سن (sinn), from Arabic سِنّ (sinn).

ety: contrac. of سنه = A. سنة sanat
def: s.f. (m.?) Year, age or period; — adv. In the year (used chiefly in dating documents, records, and the like, e.g. सन १८२३ 'in the year 1823').
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سن (sinn), from Arabic سِنّ (sinn).

def: s.m. Cloth made of hemp, coarse cloth, sacking, canvas.
adj. aff. = sā, q.v.
s.m. A whetstone, grindstone ( = sān); a touchstone.
s.m. Hemp, Cannabis sativa; — the plant Crotolaria juncea; the flax or fibre of this plant
subs: s.m. The fibre or flax of the Crotolaria juncea; string or net made of san; twine, cordage, packthread.

r: sān, sāṅ, sūdan, śā, āsā, āsūdan, sā, sher-sān
ety: said to be fr. sūdan, rt. Zend śā = S. शा, and = āsā (fr. āsūdan
def: s.m. Likeness, similitude; — muster, review (of an army; — cf. S. sainya, and H. sain)

r: san, sang, se, wā-san, wā-sang, wā-se, us-ke sang, us-se, sā, kaʼi-san, kaisā
def: 1˚ postpn. (dialec.) = sang (q.v.) and se (e.g. wā-san = wā-sang and wā-se = us-ke sang or us-se); — 2˚ aff. (dialec.) = sā, 'like,' q.v. (e.g. kaʼi-san = kaisā).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سن (sinn), from Arabic سِنّ (sinn).

def: s.m. contrac. of شني śani, q.v.
s.m. Making, creation, work, action, preparation.
s.f. Praise, eulogy, commendation, applause (it differs from ḥamd in having for its object either the Deity or man, whereas ḥamd is restricted to the Deity)
subs: adj. & s.m. Repeating praise, praising; — one who praises, praiser; s̤anā-gustar, adj. & s.m. Scattering praise; — one who scatters praise

ety: v.n. fr. سنو or سنی 'to irrigate,' &c.
def: s.m. The common senna of medicine, Cassia senna; Senna italica
subs: s.m. Senna of Mecca, Cassia lanceolata.

def: adv. Always, ever, perpetually, continually.
adj. Skilful (in the work of the hands), dexterous; industrious; fluent (of tongue), eloquent; sharp (of tongue), long (-tongued).

ety: v.n. fr. سند 'to rest or lean upon'
def: s.f. Anything on which one leans or trusts; support, prop; reliance, trust; relation, connection; seal or signature (of a judge or magistrate, &c.); order, written authority; royal ordinance, mandate, or decree, any deed or grant, &c. from one in authority, patent, grant, document, warrant, charter, commission, appointment (under which another acts), credential, diploma; testimonial, certificate; authority, precedent, example, proof, voucher; — adj. Authentic, &c. ( = sanadī, q.v.)
subs: s.f. A grant or deed conveying property from a person or family implicated in a murder, as a retaliatory fine, to the heirs of the murdered person
sanad gardānnā, v.t. To constitute (a thing) a support or authority, &c. to lean or rest on, to rely on, to trust, to believe in
(-kī), To produce, or adduce, authority (for), to quote a precedent (for), &c.
s.m. The counterpart of a grant, &c., the order to put it in execution
adj. Having proof, supported by document or proof
adj. Holding a deed, or grant, or certificate, &c.; chartered; certificated; accredited.

def: adj. Held by written deeds, &c.; chartered; accredited, authentic, reliable.
s.m. pl. (of ṣindīd, not in use in Urdū), Princes, chiefs, lords, noblemen; — calamities, misfortunes, dangers, great or formidable events.
s.m. or f. pl. (of ṣandūq, q.v.), Boxes, &c.
s.f. = A. sanā, q.v.
s.m. Rare and wonderful works of art; skilful and ingenious constructions (of language); rhetorical flourishes and ornaments; figures of speech.
s.m. A fellow-citizen.

ety: for A. سنة sanat; — see sanat and san
def: s.m. A year.
adj. corr. of sa-nāth, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.), The inflated buffalo-hide on which the natives of the hills cross rivers.
adv. See سنيه sanaih.
adv. Slowly, tardily, sluggishly; quietly, softly, mildly; stealthily; gradually, by degrees; successively; little by little, step by step
subs: s.m. The planet Saturn, or its regent.

def: adv. = شنيه śanaih, q.v.
adv. Slowly, tardily, sluggishly; quietly, softly, mildly; stealthily; gradually, by degrees; successively; little by little, step by step
subs: s.m. The planet Saturn, or its regent.

def: s.m. Name of one of the four sons of Brahmā
subs: (˚ka+ādi), s.m. The four sons of Brahmā, viz. Sanak, Sanandan, Sanātan, and Sanatkumār.

def: s.m. corr. of sanak, q.v.
v.n. To have a ringing (in the head); to become tipsy; to be turned (the head), to be or become deranged.

s.m. An idol, image (syn. but); — a mistress, lover, sweetheart; — name of a game among children (one says ṣanam āʼī, 'your sweetheart is come'; and asks 'whence?' 'in what dress?' 'what does she eat?' &c.; with whatever letter the answer to the first question begins, those to the others must begin with the same; otherwise, the person failing is condemned, by way of penalty, to imitate the voice of some animal, as a cock, or an ass, &c.)
subs: s.m. An idol-temple, a pagoda, &c. (see but-ḵẖāna).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَنَم (sanam), from Arabic صَنَم (ṣanam).

def: s.m. Name of one of the four sons of Brahmā (see sanakādi, s.v. sanak).

ety: prob. corr. of S. सन्निपात, q.v.
def: s.f. Massacre ( = sanpāt).
adv. Always, ever, continually
subs: s.m. Name of one of the four sons of Brahma, and eldest of the progenitors of mankind.

ety: for A. سنة, v.n. fr. سنو or سنه
def: s.f. A year, a single revolution of the sun.

ety: for A. صنعة, n. of un. fr. ṣanʻ, q.v.
def: s.f. Work, handiwork; art, craft, handicraft, trade, profession (syn. ḥirfa); a work of art; workmanship, skill (of a worker); make, work, manufacture, fabrication; a machine, engine; — a figure of speech; a mystery; miracle
subs: s.f. Art, artistic skill; — performance of miracles
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَنْعَت (san'at), from Arabic صَنْعَة (ṣanʕa).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Eternal, continual, perpetual; firm, fixed, permanent; primeval, ancient; — epithet of Brahma; of Vishṇu; of Śiva; name of one of the four sons of Brahma
subs: adv. From of old, from time immemorial.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Having, or accompanied by, a master or protector, having a lord or husband.
s.f. A woman whose husband is living.
adj. Of art, artistic, &c.
s.m. The pine-tree, fir, any cone-bearing tree.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَنَوْبَر (sanawbar), from Arabic صَنَوْبَر (ṣanawbar).

def: s.f. pl. (of sanat), Years; — old coin current at a depreciated rate after a certain number of years (rupees of this description are commonly called sunauṭ, in opposition to sikka rupees).
s.m. = سمبهالا sambhālā, q.v.
v.n. = سمبهلنا sambhalnā, q.v.
v.t. = sambhālnā, v.t. = سمبهالنا sambhālnā, q.v.
s.m. = سمبهالو sambhālū, q.v.
s.m. = sāmbhar, q.v.
v.t. = سمبهارنا sambhārnā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = سچ sać, q.v.
subs: prov. Fire does not harm truth; 'truth has nothing to fear' (prob. in allusion to ordeal by fire).

def: adj. = sać, and saććā, qq.v.
s.m. A mould, matrice; figure, form, outline; a cone
subs: (-ko), To cast in a mould, to mould, form, shape.

def: part. (of sanćnā) = sanćit, q.v.: — sanćā karnā = sanćit karnā.

ety: S. सञ्चलः, rt. चल् with सम्
def: s.m. (dialec.) Noise, sound of footsteps.

ety: S. सञ्चयनं, or v.n. fr. saṅćnā
def: s.m. Collecting, gathering, amassing, hoarding

s.m. Passing through, passage, progress, transition, course, going, motion; a passage, way, road, gate, doorway, entrance, opening, access; the passage or entrance of the sun (or a planet) into a new sign; — difficult progress; difficulty, distress; — setting in motion; impelling, inciting; leading, guiding; conveying, transmission, communication (of disease), contagion; — a gem supposed to be in the head of a serpent.
s.f. A female messenger, a go-between, procuress, bawd; — a pair, brace; — odour, smell.

ety: saṅćar˚ = S. सञ्चर(ति), rt. चर् with सम्
def: v.n. To go, proceed, be in motion.
s.m. Crystallized salt; Epsom salts; Glauber's salt.
s.m. Collection, gathering, heaping up, accumulation, heap, hoard, saving, store, purse, stock, capital; multitude, number, quantity
subs: (-kā), To collect, to amass, to keep in store, store up, hoard, save, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Collecting, gathering, accumulating, hoarding; — one who accumulates or hoards, amasser, hoarder.
adj. Composed, collected; attentive, mindful, conscious (of), cautious, alive (to).
adj. = saṅkshep, q.v.
s.m. = saṅkshepan, q.v.
s.m. = sākshī, q.v.
part. Heaped up, accumulated, collected, gathered, saved, hoarded; — s.m. A collection; a code
subs: v.t. To heap up, to gather up, to collect, accumulate, hoard.

def: s.f. The plant Salvinia cucullata.

ety: saṅć˚ = Prk. संच(इ) or संचे(इ) = S. सञ्चीय(ते), pass. (used actively) of rt. सम्+चि
def: v.t. To collect, gather, accumulate, heap up, save, amass, hoard.
(Braj) = sāṅćā, q.v.
s.m. = śaṇḍ, and sāṅḍ, qq.v.; — 2˚ adj. = saṇḍā.
s.m. A bull (esp. one set at liberty as an act of piety, and allowed to wander about at will); — a stallion; — an independent, extravagant fellow (syn. ćhal-ćikaniyā)
s.m. A bull at liberty; a eunuch; an impotent man; a hermaphrodite; a clown; a collection of lotus flowers; a multitude; a wood, thicket.
s.m. A rope by which a cow's legs are tied at the time of milking it.
s.m. A species of sand-lizard (com. called ūsar-sāṅḍā; — the oil extracted from it is supposed to be a cure for rheumatism, gout, and impotence).
adj. (f. -ī), Big, fat, burly, stout, strong, sturdy; — s.m. A big or fat ox; — hardened excrement, scybala
subs: v.n. To become fat and big, to grow stout and sturdy, &c. ( = saṇḍiyānā)
or saṇḍ-musaṇḍ, adj. Fat, plump, well-developed, stout, &c. ( = saṇḍā).

def: s.m. An anvil ( = sandān, q.v.); a broad stone; fetid excrement ( = sanḍā, q.v.).
s.m. = صندل ṣandal, q.v.
s.m. Sandal-wood, sanders; — a perfumed embrocation obtained by rubbing a piece of sandal-wood with water on a stone (called ṣandalāsā)
subs: vulg. ṣandlāsā, s.m. A flat circular stone on which sandal-wood is ground
s.f. = ṣandal-āsā, q.v.; — (and) a slab for grinding spices or drugs on
(kisī-ke), 'To have marks of ground sandal-wood made on the face'; to be distinguished; to come off with honour or with flying colours.

def: s.m. A dealer in sandal-wood a seller of perfumes and drugs.
s.m. A flat stone on which sandal-wood is ground ( = ṣandlāsā).
adj. & s.f. = صندلي ṣandalī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Made of sandal-wood; of the colour of sandal-wood, light-yellow; anything coloured with sandal-wood; — a eunuch whose parts of generation have been entirely extracted.

r: ṣandalī, ṣandlī, ṣandal, sandal, lit., sandalī
def: (fr. ṣandal, for orig. sandal, 'a sandal, a boot'), s.f. lit. 'The place for the sandals or boots'; a bench, a seat; a movable scaffold; (it is said to have been the custom in old times to place the boots or shoes of princes and nobles on a bench in an ante-chamber; whence the term sandalī).
s.m. Rope, cord (esp. for tying cattle); — an elephant's temples, or the part whence the ichorous fluid issues.

ety: prob. = S. सन्धान; but said to be A., and to be derived fr. سند; see sanad
def: s.f. An anvil; the iron on which the knocker of a door strikes.
s.f. A cow-house.
s.m. Pincers, &c. ( = saṅṛsī, q.v.).

ety: A. sandarūs; Gr. σαϜδαράχη; P. sandara; S. सिन्टूरः
def: s.m. Sandarach, a resin obtained from Thuja articulata or Callitris quadrivalvis (a tree growing in Barbary); — the proto-sulphuret of arsenic, red arsenic, realgar.

s.m. Looking steadfastly, gazing, viewing, beholding, seeing, sight, vision; seeing, one another, meeting; appearance, presence; regard, consideration, reflection; — causing to see, showing, presenting.

s.m. Looking steadfastly, gazing, viewing, beholding, seeing, sight, vision; seeing, one another, meeting; appearance, presence; regard, consideration, reflection; — causing to see, showing, presenting.
s.m. A cesspool, latrine, necessary, water-closet (commonly on the roof of a house, and communicating with a pool or drain under the house); a sink, sewer.
s.m. A pair of tongs or nippers, large pincers.
s.f. A pair of pincers or nippers, small tongs, forceps (see saṅṛsī).
s.m. corr. of sannyāsī, q.v.
s.m. (dialec.) = sandā and sanḍsī, qq.v.
s.m. Doubt,uncertainty, suspense, hesitation, apprehension, mistrust, suspicion, anxiety; risk, danger; grief, sorrow
subs: (-meṅ, or -kā?), To doubt; to be apprehensive (about); &c.

def: adj. & s.m. Doubtful, dubious; doubting; suspicious, mistrustful, apprehensive; scrupulous; — a doubter; a mistrustful person, &c.
s.m. (dialec.) = sandeh; — adj. = sandehī.
s.m. See سنديس sandes.
s.m. Information, news, tidings, intelligence, report; message, commission, command, direction; embassy; a present.
s.m. Information, news, tidings, intelligence, report; message, commission, command, direction; embassy; a present.
s.m. = sandesī, q.v.
s.m. (f. -inī), One who brings news, a messenger, an envoy, an ambassador.
s.m. (dialec.) = sandes, and sandesī, qq.v.
s.m. = sandesu.
s.m. = sandhaw, q.v.

s.m. The act of placing or holding together, joining, junction, union, tying, binding, fixing (an arrow to a bow, &c.), arranging; association, league, combination, alliance; companionship, company; peace, repairing, restoration; composing, compounding, mixing, preparation; drawing together, contraction (as of the skin by astringents. &c.); advertence, attention; sustaining, supporting; reception, receiving; — an aim; guess, discovery; search, inquiry, prying into secrets, spying; a secret; information about a lost thing or person; — distillation; spirituous liquor; pickles; a relish, something eaten to excite thirst; sour rice-gruel
subs: (-kā), To obtain a clue (to), to track, trace, discover
v.t. To arrange; to prepare; to fix (an arrow to a bow), to take aim, &c.

def: s.m. = H سندهاني सन्धानी sandhānī[S.सन्धानिका], s.f. Pickle, acid preparation of bel and other fruits.
s.f. = H سندهانا सन्धाना sandhānā [S.सन्धान+कः], s.m. Pickle, acid preparation of bel and other fruits.
s.f. Distilling, distillation; — a foundry, a place where metals are wrought or stored.
s.m. A kind of salt, rock-salt.

s.f. Conjunction, junction, combination, union, contact; composition, construction, structure; compact, agreement, association, alliance, league, company; reconciliation, peace, making a treaty of peace, negotiating an alliance, pacification; a treaty; (in Gram.) euphonic combination or coalescence of the final letter of a word with the initial letter of a following word; a connecting link; a joint, articulation (of the body, &c.); a fold; an interval, pause, rest, stop; — critical juncture, opportune moment; — dividing, breaking; division, separation; breach, chasm, mine, hole, opening or cavity made in a wall or underneath a building (by a housebreaker, &c.); vagina, vulva; a period at the expiration of each yuga or age (equivalent to one-sixth of its duration, and intervening before the commencement of the next)
subs: s.m. One who brings about union, or pacification, &c.; reconciler, peace-maker.

def: part. Joined or fastened together, bound, tied, united, strung; fixed, fitted, prepared; reconciled, allied.
v.t. (dialec.) = sādhnā, q.v.

s.f. Mean (between two things); meeting of day and night, twilight (see sāṅjh and sanjhā); a period of time, forenoon, mid-day, afternoon, or evening; holy rites of morning, noon, and evening; evening prayer; — joining, union; promise, agreement; boundary, limit; the period between the expiration of one age and the commencement of another (see sandhi); — reflection, meditation; name of a flower
subs: or sandhyā-kāl, s.m. Eventide, twilight, dusk
To pray at sunrise, noon, or sunset.

def: part. adj. Confused, indistinct, obscure, unintelligible; doubted, questioned, dubious, doubtful, uncertain; entertaining doubt, unsettled, desponding; unsafe, dangerous, riskful
subs: s.m. (in gram.) The past-dubious tense (e.g. tum-ne dekhā hogā, 'You may have seen,' 'it may be that you saw')
s.m. The present-dubious tense (e.g. woh suntī hogī, 'it may be that she hears,' 'she may be hearing').

def: s.m. = sandīpan, q.v.
s.m. Lighting up, kindling, inflaming, igniting; inspiriting, encouraging, encouragement; — name of one of the arrows of Kām-dev.
s.m. = sāndīpani, q.v.
s.m. Name of a Muni or sage who was the tutor of Krishn and Balarām.
s.m. Name of a Muni or sage who was the tutor of Krishn and Balarām.
s.m. A young camel; — a wheel used in lace-making.
v.n. To become fat and big, to grow strong and burly, or stout and lusty.
s.f. A female camel (for riding), a dromedary (cf. sār and sārnī)
subs: s.m. One who is mounted on a camel; a camel-rider; a messenger (who comes on a camel).

def: s.m. Quantity, mass, assemblage, multitude, concourse.
s.f. A pair of pincers or nippers, small tongs, forceps (see saṅṛsī).
s.m. corr. of صندوقچه ṣandūqća, q.v.
= H سندوکچا सन्दूकचा sandūkćā s.m. corr. of صندوق; and صندوقچه; qq.v.
= H سندوك सन्दूक sandūk, s.m. corr. of صندوق; and صندوقچه; qq.v.
s.m. = H صندوقچي ṣandūq-ćī, s.f. A small box or chest; a casket.
s.f. = P صندوقچه ṣandūq-ća, s.m. A small box or chest; a casket.
adj. Like a box, box-shaped (applied to a tomb; or the scabbard of a sword, &c.); oblong (as a pillar).
s.m. A small, sweet-smelling, red-streaked mango.
s.m. The act of vitiating or corrupting; any vice which causes infamy.

ety: act. part. of صنع 'to make, construct,' &c.
def: s.m. A maker, manufacturer, an artificer, artisan; the Creator
s.m. Oil, unguent, grease, fat; oiliness, unctuousness, greasiness, viscidity; blandness, tenderness, love, affection, kindness
subs: (˚ha+ar˚), adv. With a view to love, on account of love, for love's sake
(-kā), To form an attachment (for), to become mutually attached
adj. (f. -vatī), Oily, unctuous; possessed of affection, affectionate, loving.

def: s.m. (poet.) = snehī.
s.f. Spear, javelin, dart (all of iron), a goad; — a borer for wells.

ety: S. सङ्गे, loc. of सङ्ग as above
def: adj. Of equal weight, equal in weight
subs: (-ke), To join or attach (oneself to); to follow
(-ko), To take (one) into the company (of, apne).

def: s.m. Attachment, devotion (to), fondness, addition (to), predilection, propensity (for); affection, desire, wish, cupidity; copulation.

ety: prob. akin to sanj, q.v.; or Pehl. śang; Zend aśan; S. aśan
def: s.m. A stone; weight
subs: s.m. The black stone in the Kaʻba of Mecca
s.m. Showering stones; — a stony place
s.m. Pelting with stones; a shower of stones
s.m. A kind of white stone; 'the white stone,' zinc
s.m. A stone to rub the feet with when bathing
or saṅg-pusht, or saṅg-post, s.m. A tortoise; a turtle
or saṅg-ḵẖārā, s.m. Hard stone; flint
or saṅg-ḵẖẉāṛak, s.m. 'Stone-swallower,' a kind of bird, the rail (A. syn. qat̤ā)
adj. & s.m. Stony-hearted, hard-hearted, unfeeling, cruel; obdurate; — a hard-hearted person, an unfeeling wretch
s.m. and saṅg-rezī, s.f. Gravel; a pebble
s.m. Stoning (to death); — one who stones (to death); — adj. & s.m. Abounding in stone; — a stony place
s.f. The act of stoning to death
s.m. Worker in or on stone; one who makes corrections on the lithographic stone
s.f. Washing the stones or grit out of rice before dressing
or saṅg-kūpī, s.f. The plant Volkameria inermis (used in medicine)
adj. Abounding in stone, stony, rocky; arduous
s.m. A black close-grained stone so called; black slate
s.m. A mark to throw stones at
corr. saṅg-ě-yashab, s.m. A kind of jasper or agate.

def: s.m. Coming together, joining, uniting, meeting, encountering, concurrence, confluence (of rivers); contact, touch; coherence; union, association, connection, intimacy, friendship, love; keeping company (with), intercourse, company, society; a company, body, band, caravan
subs: s.m. Very great intimacy; — adv. On terms of very close friendship
(-kā), To leave or give up the company (of), to withdraw (from)
(-kā), To keep company (with), associate (with), have intercourse or dealings (with)

def: adj. Having all the members, complete in all its parts, finished; possessed of the auxiliary branches (aṅga) of sacred science
subs: (˚ga+up˚), adj. Entire, complete; along with the aṅgas and upāṅgas or the principal and auxiliary branches of sacred science.

def: s.m. Imitation, acting, mimicry, disguise, impersonation; mockery, sham, farce; a play, representation; a scene, show; a part in a play, a character
subs: To get up a play or entertainment
v.n. To act; to play the fool, make a buffoon of oneself
To act the part (of, -kā), to represent a character, to act; to play the fool; to sham, to pretend
To represent a character; to sham, to pretend
To act, to play a part; to act foolishly, to play the fool
(-kā), To imitate, to mimic, to take off; to act the part (of)
To act the buffoon, to play the fool; to skip and jump about, to cut capers

def: s.m. (dialec.) = saṅg, 'company,' q.v.

s.m. Meeting, encounter; union, junction, conjunction (of planets), confluence (of rivers); contact, touch; sexual intercourse, coition; association, society, company; fitness, adaptation, appropriateness; mixture
ex: یا الہ آباد میں رہیے جہاں سنگم بھی ہو یا بنارس میں جہاں ہر گھاٹ پر سیلاب ہے
yā ilāhābād mẽ rahie jahā̃ saṅgam bhī ho yā banāras mẽ jahā̃ har ghāṭ par sailāb hai
Stay either in Allahabad where there is confluence, or in Varanasi where there is flood at every pier.

def: s.m. See s.v. sān, 'whetstone.'
s.m. The pod of the Śami tree; a kind of bean; the pod or fruit of the Janṭ tree.
s.m. Agreement, assent; promise; a bargain, transaction of sale; knowledge; — misfortune, calamity; poison; conflict, war, battle; intrenchment, lines; — the fruit of the śamī-tree.
s.f. = saṅgati, q.v.
part. Come together, convened, met, encountered; joined, conjoined, united, associated; assembled, collected, accumulated, mixed; fitted, apposite, proper, appropriate.
s.f. = saṅghātin, q.v.
s.m. = saṅghātī, q.v.

s.f. Meeting, interview, joining, union, junction, conjunction, mixture; association, intercourse, company, companionship, acquaintance, friendship; sexual intercourse, coition; a collection, congregation, assembly, an association, a society, party, fraternity; adaptation, fitness, appropriateness, applicability; going to, visiting, frequenting; meeting by chance; coming to pass accidentally; chance, accident; questioning for further information; a place where the Sikhs celebrate their religious ceremonies; a place of worship; a monastery; instrumental accompaniment
subs: (or saṅgat) karnā (-kī), To keep company (with), to associate (with).

def: s.m. Associate, companion, comrade; one of a company or party.
s.m. Associate, companion, comrade; one of a company or party.
adv. = saṅg, q.v.
s.m. Collection, assemblage, multitude, crowd, host, band, group, flock, herd, shoal, number, heap, quantity.
s.m. A wife's son by a former husband, step-son.

ety: Prk. संघारो or संहारो; S. संहारः
def: s.m. Contraction, compression; abbreviation, abridgment; comprehensive description; practice, skill; restraining; suppressing; murdering, killing, slaughter, carnage; destruction, loss, ruin; — the destruction of the world at the end of a kalp; name of one of the divisions of the infernal regions; — adj. Killed, destroyed.
s.m. Intrenchment, lines ( = saṅgar, q.v.).

ety: prob. fr. sanghār; — or saṅghār˚ = Prk. संघार(इ) or संघारे(इ) = S. संहारय (ति), caus. of rt. सम्+हृ
def: v.t. To destroy, to kill; to make away with; to consume, to devour.
s.m. Rubbing together, friction; grinding, trituration; collision; clashing, rivalry, emulation, envy; — going gently, gliding, flowing.
s.m. Rubbing together, friction; grinding, trituration; collision; clashing, rivalry, emulation, envy; — going gently, gliding, flowing.
adj. & s.m. Emulating, rivalling; — one who vies (with), emulator, rival.

s.m. Union, combination, association, confederacy; an assemblage, a collection, quantity, heap, cluster, multitude, band; society, companionship; collection of mucus, phlegm; composition of words, formation of compounds; — striking, killing, hurting; — name of a division of the infernal regions.
adj. = saṅhat, q.v.
s.f. = saṅhati, q.v
s.m. Associate, companion, comrade, friend, ally; an accessory.
s.f. A bawd, procuress.
s.f. Female companion, a woman's companion or associate.
s.f. = saṅhatatā, q.v.

s.m. Rubbing together, friction; clashing together, collision, shock, clash, encounter, conflict; meeting, joining together, cohesion, union, close contact, concurrence, conflux; embracing; embrace; intertwining of wrestlers; — a pimp.
s.m. = S سنگهٿنا सङ्घट्टना, संघट्टना saṅ-ghaṭṭānā, and H. संघटना saṅghaṭanā, s.f. = saṅghaṭṭa, q.v.
s.f. = S سنگهٿن सङ्घट्टना, संघट्टना saṅ-ghaṭṭan,and H. संघटन saṅghaṭan, s.m. = saṅghaṭṭa, q.v.
s.f. A bawd, procuress ( = saṅ-ghāṭikā).
s.f. = saṅhitā, q.v.
s.m. Associate, companion ( = next).
s.m. Mimic, actor, player.
s.f. A support on which the pole of a cart is propped; — the coach-box (of a hative vehicle); the frame-work of a native cart (over which a covering or curtain is placed to secure privacy for women).
s.f. Striped silk cloth.
adj. & s.m. Going with, uniting with, connected with; — associate, companion, comrade; confederate, ally, accomplice
adj. Stony; of stone.
adj. Attached, devoted, addicted (to), fond (of); intent (on), studious; full of affection or desire, desirous; lustful, libidinous.

ety: saṅg, q.v.+īn = Zend aff. aêna = S. इन्
def: adj. Stony, of stone, made of stone; hard; firm, solid; strong; thick, stout, close-woven; heavy, weighty; grave, serious; severe (e.g. sazā-ě-saṅgīn, 'severe punishment'), excessive; — s.f. A bayonet (in this sense supposed to be derived from Turkish süngyü)

ety: saṅgīn, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Heaviness; a load; firmness, solidity; hardness, severity; excessiveness, excess.
s.m. = sangīt, q.v.
s.m. A song in chorus, chorus; a public entertainment consisting of song, music, and dancing, a concert; the art of singing with music and dancing
subs: s.m. lit. 'Mirror of harmony,' name of a work on music

def: s.m. (dialec.) = saṅg, q.v.
s.m. See s.v. saṅg, 'stone.'
s.f. (local) See s.v. saṅg, 'stone.'
s.m. (dialec.) = sāṅkho, q.v.
s.m. Hiding, concealing, complete concealment.

ety: saṅgor˚ = S. सङ्गूढ, p.p.p. of rt. सम्+गुह्
def: v.t. To collect, store, lay up, amass, hoard.
s.m. See s.v. saṅg, 'stone.'

s.m. Taking, seizing; grasping; clenching (the fist); reception, admission, assent; guarding, protecting, protection, favouring, supporting, encouraging; propitiation; entertainment; bringing together, conjunction, collection, compilation, code, catalogue, abridgement, epitome, summary; sum, amount, totality; restraining, controlling, governing; effort, exertion; mentioning, mention; greatness; elevation, loftiness
subs: and saṅgrah-karne-hārā, s.m. Seizer; collector, compiler, &c.

def: s.m. Taking hold of, seizing, grasping; taking, accepting, receiving, admitting; sexual intercourse, adultery; bringing together, collecting, compiling; mixing, blending; hoping, hope; inlaying, enchasing.
s.f. Irregular state of the bowels, constipation alternately with diarrhœa; diarrhœa; dysentery; cramp (as in cholera).
s.m. Collector, compiler.
part. Received, taken, accepted; imparted, bestowed; collected, compiled.
s.m. War, battle, fighting, fight, tussle, scuffle
subs: s.f. Battlefield, region or locality of war.

def: s.m. See s.v. saṅg, 'stone.'
s.f. See s.v. saṅg, 'stone.'
s.f. corr. of saṅsī, q.v.
s.m. corr. of saṅskār, q.v.

ety: supposed to be a corr. of Port. cintra
def: s.m. A kind of orange; (met.) breast of a woman.

ety: supposed to be a corr. of Port. cintra
def: s.m. A kind of orange; (met.) breast of a woman.
s.f. The skate-fish, Raia sankur.
s.f. The skate-fish, Raia sankur.

ety: saṅgwā˚ = S. संगमय (ति), caus. of rt. गम् with सम्
def: v.t. To collect, gather together, bring together; to put in order, to arrange; to attain; to catch; to take possession of; to cause to die, to kill
s.f. A noun; a metaphor, an idiomatical phrase, &c.
s.m. Collection, &c. (see saṅghār, the pop. form).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Contracting, compressing, abridging, abbreviating; destructive, ruinous; — destroyer, ruiner, &c.
adj. & s.m. = saṅhārak, q.v.
part. Destroyed, slain, killed, murdered.

ety: saṅhār˚ = Prk. संहार(इ) or संहारे(इ) = S. संहारय(ति), caus. of rt. सम्+हृ
def: v.t. To destroy, &c. ( = saṅghārnā, q.v.).
s.m. Name of one of the divisions of hell.

part. Struck together, closely joined or united, closed, shut; close, firm, compact, solid; closely allied, combined; joined, connected; heaped up, collected, assembled, accumulated; struck, hurt, wounded, killed.
s.f. Close approximation; contact; compactness; combination, union; agreement.
s.f. Close contact, close combination, firm union or alliance, junction; compactness; bulk; a collection, assemblage, heap, multitude.
s.f. Mark, sign, token.

s.f. Collection, compilation, compendium; complete collection of the hymns and invocations of any one of the Vedas; arrangement of the texts of the Vedas into short sentences; (in Gram.) the coalescence of the final letter of a word with the initial letter of a following word.
s.f. Shouting, calling out together, general shout, clamour, exclamation, tumultuous exultation.
s.m.f. Second; — a second; match, equal
subs: adv. A second or another time, secondly

def: s.m. The planet Saturn
s.f. Hemp (of a finer quality than san), fine hemp.
s.f. A hempen cloth or garment; a new, unseamed, and single breadth of cloth (given to the religious student at his investiture for his upper garment); a ragged garment.
s.f. A whetstone, &c. ( = śān, q.v.).
s.f. Saturday evening
s.m. Giving, gift, donation; service, worship; — request, &c. ( = next, q.v.).
postpn. (dialec.) = sang, q.v.
s.f. An imitative sound (as of the the wind, &c.)
subs: s.f. Soughing or whistling (of the wind); whizzing; rustling, rustle; — creeping or tingling sensation.

def: s.f. Request, solicitation, respectful entreaty; — quarter, region, point of the compass.
s.f. corr. of sānī, q.v.
s.m. Master, lord; husband; the Supreme Being, God; — a religious mendicant.
s.f. Chaff or chopped straw mixed with grain (esp. that from which oil has been expressed) as food for cattle; mash; — s.m. A caste of cultivators.

ety: Prk. सण्मिच्छरो; S. शनैश्र्चरः or शनैस्+चरः
def: s.m. (lit. 'slow-going'), The planet Saturn; — Saturday; (met.) ill-luck; poverty, penury; dirt; dirty clothes; a dirty, ill-looking fellow; a miser; a glutton
subs: (kisī-ke sir), The evil influence of Saturn to come upon (the head of anyone); to come under the influence of an evil star, to suffer misfortune or ill-luck.

def: s.m. corr. of sanīćar, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Of or relating to Saturn, or to Saturday; unlucky; — an unlucky man, a miserable wretch, &c. (see sanīćar); — name of a mountain near Goāliyār.

ety: for A. سانحة saniḥat, fem. or n. of un. fr. sāniḥ, act. part. of سنح 'to present itself, to occur'
def: s.m. An occurrence, event, incident.
s.m. pl. of next, q.v.

adj. Sharpened, whetted, made sharp-pointed; sharp; — s.m. What flows (from the whetstone) on the occasion of sharpening iron (or a knife, &c.); what falls from a stone on its being rubbed or grated upon another stone; filings, gratings, &c.
s.f. An imitative sound (as of the the wind, &c.)
subs: s.f. Soughing or whistling (of the wind); whizzing; rustling, rustle; — creeping or tingling sensation.

def: s.f. corr. of sangīn, q.v.
s.m. Master, lord; husband; the Supreme Being, God; — a religious mendicant.
s.m. Name of a bank or alluvial island in the middle of the river Son; — an insular spot enclosed by the branches of the river Sarjū at the point where it falls into the Ganges above Chaprā.
s.m. = sanīćar, q.v.
s.m. lit. 'Slow-moving'; the planet Saturn; — Saturday ( = sanīćar, q.v.).
s.m. See s.v. سني sani.
s.m. A kind of tasar cloth.

ety: for A. ثانية s̤āniyat, fem. of s̤ānī
def: adj. & s.f. Second; — a second, a moment.
adv. Secondly, in the second place.

ety: rt. of sanjīdan; fr. Zend śać = S. शक्
def: part. & s.m. Weighing, measuring; — weigher, measurer; examiner (used as last member of compounds, e.g. nagma-sanj or tarāna-sanj, s.m. A measurer of sounds, i.e. a musician; — sukhan-sanj, s.m. A weigher of words; an orator; a poet).
s.m. corr. of sāj, q.v.
s.f. contrac. of sāṅjh, q.v.
s.m. The ermine, the miniver; the grey squirrel; ermine, fur.
